steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Data is trapped on obsolete technology and can no longer be read. A data-transfer project must do something to that data. The situation calls for a word meaning to recover something valuable from loss — not to fix its quality.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — improve: assumes the data itself is flawed and needs enhancement. The problem is accessibility, not quality. ✗
  • Choice B — simplify: suggests making the data less complex. That is unrelated to recovering it from inaccessible storage. ✗
  • Choice C — amend: means to correct or revise. The data does not need correction — it needs extraction. ✗
  • Choice D — salvage: means to recover something valuable from being lost or destroyed. Exactly what a data-transfer project does with data stuck on dead formats. ✓
D is correct.
